summary:
Interleukin-1 (IL-1) plays a fundamental role in initiating periapical inflammation, serving as a primary driver of the inflammatory cascade. Additional cytokines such as interleukin-6 (IL-6) promote osteoclastic activity and bone resorption, while interleukin-8 (IL-8) recruits neutrophils contributing to tissue damage; however, IL-1 remains centrally implicated due to its well-documented initiation and sustained regulatory effects. 

\boxed{A}  

(Note: This summary adheres strictly to only clinically relevant facts derived from the provided rationale—no external or unmentioned content.)  
--- 
End of evaluation.
